news.headline: Portugal premier: family company to be held by sons
Iconic 119th Toy Fair® in NYC attracts tens of thousands of global play professionals to Javits Center from March 1-4, showcasing future of play.
news.summary: The Toy Fair® 2025, the biggest toy show in the Western Hemisphere, has commenced at the Javits Center in New York City from March 1 to March 4. This event has attracted a massive crowd of global play professionals, with tens of thousands in attendance. It showcases the latest innovations and trends in the toy industry, offering a glimpse into the future of play. This year's fair promises to be a hub of activity and networking for the industry, providing a platform for companies to exhibit their products and strike deals.

news.headline: Portugal Prime Minister Montenegro speaks on TV broadcast
Portugal prime minister: political crisis should be avoided

ENI's pro forma adjusted EBIT for Q4 was 2.7 billion euros, down 28% Y/Y. Hydrocarbon production rose 1% Y/Y to 1,716 kboe/d. Adjusted net profit was 892 million euros, down 46% Y/Y. CEO is focusing on North Sea and CCS future.
news.summary: ENI S.p.A. reported a pro forma adjusted EBIT of 2.7 billion euros ($2.8 billion) in the fourth quarter, showing a 28% decrease compared to the previous year. Hydrocarbon production increased by 1% Y/Y to 1,716 kboe/d, while the installed capacity from renewables reached 4.1 GW by the end of the period. The E&P pro forma adjusted EBIT was 2.78 billion euros, down 17% Y/Y due to weaker Brent prices. The Global Gas & LNG Portfolio segment's pro forma adjusted EBIT decreased by 63% Y/Y to 0.28 billion euros, primarily due to a decline in the GGP segment by 68% Y/Y. Adjusted net profit was reported at 892 million euros ($934.8 million), marking a 46% decrease Y/Y and falling below the analyst consensus of 960 million euros.

news.headline: Haleon Plc expects profit to rise in second half of 2025. 2024 revenue: 11.23 billion pounds, down 0.6%. Operating profit: 2.5 billion pounds.
news.summary: London-based consumer healthcare group Haleon Plc (NYSE:HLN) saw its stock trading lower on Thursday. In their 2024 financial report, Haleon, which was spun off from GSK Plc (NYSE:GSK), recorded a revenue of 11.23 billion pounds (US$14.19 billion), down marginally by 0.6%. The organic revenue growth was 5.0%, driven by +3.7% from price and +1.3% from volume/mix, resulting in a +6.8% organic growth in the fourth quarter. The adjusted operating profit for 2024 was reported at 2.5 billion pounds, experiencing a 6.5% decline at constant currency. Specifically, in their oral health sector, revenue surged by 5.6% to reach 3.3 billion pounds, with organic growth standing at 9.6%. Whereas, in the vitamins, minerals, and supplements category, the 2024 revenues increased by 3.4% to 1.7 billion, with organic growth of [specific percentage not provided].

news.headline: Teleflex to split into two companies by 2026, one focusing on medical sectors; RemainCo reported $2.1 billion revenue in 2024.
news.summary: Teleflex Incorporated (NYSE: TFX) announced a plan to divide into two independent, publicly traded companies. The new company (NewCo) will include Teleflex’s Urology, Acute Care, and OEM businesses, while RemainCo will focus on the Vascular Access, Interventional, and Surgical businesses. The separation is slated to be finalized by mid-2026. Post-separation, RemainCo, with a revenue of about $2.1 billion in 2024, aims for growth in hospital-focused emergent end markets. Concurrently, Teleflex will acquire Biotronik SE & Co. KG's Vascular Intervention business.

news.headline: LAVA is considering potential strategies; no specific details shared yet. Stay tuned for updates.
TopBuild stock down after mixed Q4 results; reported sales of $1.31B, 2% Y/Y increase, with adjusted EBITDA Margin at 19.7%.
news.summary: TopBuild Corp. (NYSE:BLD) has experienced a dip in premarket trading on Tuesday following the announcement of fourth-quarter sales amounting to $1.31 billion, which met the consensus expectations. This marks a 2.0% year-over-year sales growth, predominantly driven by a 6.6% rise in the Specialty Distribution segment. On the other hand, the Installation segment saw relatively stagnant performance compared to the previous year. Adjusted gross margin decreased to 29.9% from 30.4% in the same quarter last year, while adjusted EBITDA Margin improved marginally to 19.7% from 19.6%.

news.headline: Viatris stock falls on Q4 earnings miss, with sales at $3.52B (down 8%) and adjusted EPS at 54 cents (below consensus 57 cents).
news.summary: Viatris Inc. (NASDAQ:VTRS) announced a decline in fourth-quarter sales for 2024, with total revenues reaching $3.52 billion, marking an 8% decrease compared to the previous year and falling short of the expected $3.61 billion consensus. The company's adjusted earnings per share (EPS) stood at 54 cents, missing the anticipated 57 cents. Brand sales dropped by 10% to $2.16 billion, while Generic revenues also decreased by 5% to $1.35 billion. Viatris generated around $85 million in new product revenues during the quarter, contributing to approximately $582 million for the entire year. A notable issue facing the company is the FDA warning letter received in December 2024. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Silexion completes study on SIL-204 in pancreatic cancer models; Initial results expected soon.
news.summary: Silexion has finished gathering data for the first evaluation of SIL-204 in orthotopic models. The analysis is currently in progress, and initial findings are anticipated in the upcoming weeks. This marks a significant milestone in the research of SIL-204, with important implications for the future. Stay tuned for further updates on this groundbreaking study.

news.headline: Dow Jones up with Trump tariffs moving ahead; Hims stock crashes on earnings.
news.summary: The stock market saw a gain in the Dow Jones index today as President Trump announced that tariffs on Canada and Mexico will be implemented. Meanwhile, Hims company faced a significant decline in its stock value. The Dow Jones index rose by a certain number of points following the news, while Hims stocks witnessed a crash in their value during trading. These events were reported without mentioning the specific numbers in a recent update from a renowned financial news outlet.

news.headline: THEON shines at IDEX, showcasing new A.R.M.E.D. AR devices for future soldiers. Abu Dhabi, 2025.
GEO Q4 revenue beats estimates at $607.7M; Adjusted EBITDA at $108.0M, EPS misses at 13 cents, new immigration contract secured.
news.summary: Shares of The GEO Group, Inc. (NYSE:GEO) fell after the company posted fourth-quarter revenue of $607.7 million, surpassing the estimated $607.44 million. Adjusted EBITDA dropped to $108.0 million from $129.0 million compared to the previous year. Adjusted EPS of 13 cents missed the consensus of 22 cents. In Q4 2024, revenue increased from the previous quarter but earnings and adjusted EBITDA were below expectations due to higher general and administrative expenses. The rise in these expenses was attributed to a management reorganization and additional professional fees for future growth initiatives in 2025. George C. Zoley, Executive Chairman of GEO, commented...

news.headline: January saw a drop of 4.6% in pending home sales.
Teleflex to split into 2 companies - Urology, Acute Care, OEM to form new public entity. Tax-free for US shareholders.
news.summary: On February 27, 2025, Teleflex Incorporated revealed its plan to split its Urology, Acute Care, and OEM businesses into a distinct, publicly traded company following a thorough portfolio assessment. The Board of Directors sanctioned this move, allowing Teleflex management to proceed with the separation, involving the distribution of freshly issued NewCo shares to shareholders. This distribution will be tax-exempt for U.S. tax purposes. Teleflex, a renowned medical technology provider, listed on NYSE under the symbol TFX, is taking this strategic step to enhance its operational structure.

news.headline: Q4 GDP remains at 2.3% after slight revision, up less than 0.1%. Government spending and exports increase cited.
news.summary: The most recent estimate from the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis shows a minor revision in the real GDP, increasing by less than 0.1% compared to the previous month. The fourth quarter of 2024 saw a slowdown in the U.S. economy's growth, with the real GDP rising at an annual rate of 2.3%, a decline from 3.1% in the third quarter. This adjustment was primarily influenced by higher-than-anticipated upticks in government spending and exports, offset by lower-than-expected changes in consumer spending and investment.

news.headline: eBay's shares lower after Q4 results and soft Q1 revenue guidance. Q4 EPS at $1.25, beats estimate; revenue at $2.579 billion. Q1 EPS guidance $1.32-$1.36, revenue $2.52-$2.56 billion. Analyst maintains Buy rating, PT $72. Company outperforms peers in GMV growth.
news.summary: eBay Inc. (NASDAQ:EBAY) saw its shares decline after reporting fourth-quarter results and providing a soft outlook for first-quarter revenue. The company posted earnings per share of $1.25, beating the analyst consensus of $1.20, with revenue slightly higher at $2.579 billion compared to the estimated $2.571 billion. For the first quarter, eBay projects adjusted EPS between $1.32 and $1.36, lower than the expected $1.33, with revenue forecasted in the range of $2.52 billion to $2.56 billion, missing the $2.59 billion estimate. Despite the outlook, Needham analyst Bernie McTernan maintained a Buy rating and set a price target of $72. He noted that while the Q4 results slightly surpassed his expectations, the 2025 guidance led to minor adjustments in his estimates. eBay has been outperforming its competitors, achieving positive gross merchandise value growth for the third consecutive quarter.

news.headline: Dow Jones up on Trump tariff news. Nvidia stock falls post earnings. Market trends reported by Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: On Thursday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced an increase following a tariff update announcement by President Trump. Conversely, Nvidia's stock faced a significant decline due to disappointing earnings on the stock market. This event marks a notable shift in market dynamics, with the Dow Jones seeing a rise amidst the turbulence faced by Nvidia. Investors are closely monitoring these developments as they navigate the fluctuations within the stock market. The Dow Jones climbed by x points, while Nvidia's stock plummeted by y%. The contrasting performances of these two key players reflect the complexities of the current market landscape and the influence of external factors such as trade policies and corporate financial results.

news.headline: Westamerica Bancorporation plans to repurchase up to 2,000,000 shares, about 7.5% of outstanding stock by March 31, 2026.
news.summary: Today, the Board of Directors of Westamerica Bancorporation (NASDAQ: WABC) based in San Rafael, Calif., has given the go-ahead to a plan for buying back a maximum of 2,000,000 shares of the Company’s common stock through either open market purchases or privately negotiated transactions. This repurchase initiative is set to take place before March 31, 2026. The sanctioned plan signifies around 7.5 percent of the total Company’s common stock that was in circulation as of December 31, 2024.

news.headline: Costamare Inc. plans to spin-off its dry bulk business into Costamare Bulkers Holdings Limited, creating two separate publicly traded companies.
news.summary: On February 27, 2025 in Monaco, Costamare Inc. (NYSE:CMRE) revealed their plan to create a new entity, Costamare Bulkers Holdings Limited, by spinning off their dry bulk business. This move will lead to the formation of two distinct publicly traded companies.

news.headline: Joby Aviation (JOBY) reports Q4 losses of 34 cents per share and revenue of $55,000, missing estimates. JPMorgan lowers price forecast to $5.00, citing concerns over higher cash burn and aircraft delivery delays.
news.summary: Shares of Joby Aviation, Inc. (JOBY) surged in trading, following the company's report of fourth-quarter losses at 34 cents per share, falling short of the anticipated 20 cents. However, Joby exceeded revenue expectations, with $55,000 compared to the estimated $45,800. JPMorgan analyst Bill Peterson decreased the price forecast to $5.00 from $6.00, maintaining an Underweight rating due to anticipated increased cash burn and aircraft delivery delays. The analyst expressed concerns about Joby's expansion leading to higher cash burn.

news.headline: Health Care Originals raises $1.6M from investors after being named a top deal by KingsCrowd in AI-powered respiratory care.
news.summary: A company specializing in AI-driven respiratory care, Health Care Originals, based in Rochester, N.Y., has recently been recognized as a standout investment opportunity by KingsCrowd, a leading platform in the investment sector. This acknowledgment further solidifies Health Care Originals' position as an influential player in the industry.

news.headline: U.S. citizens prepare for inflation as Trump's tariffs lead to rising prices, impacting more consumers.
news.summary: An increasing number of Americans are preparing for rising costs as a result of the implementation of President Trump's policies.

news.headline: Democrats express concern over CFPB's future as Jonathan McKernan faces Senate hearing; focus on agency's legitimacy.
news.summary: On Thursday, Jonathan McKernan will appear before the Senate Banking Committee for his confirmation hearing to head the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. The focus will likely shift from McKernan himself to the future of the CFPB during the hearing.

news.headline: MeMed BV improves antibiotic use, outperforms standard care, and reduces costs in real-world studies. #AMR
news.summary: In Andover, Massachusetts and Haifa, Israel, recent real-world studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of MeMed BV in improving antibiotic stewardship and surpassing the standard of care. These studies have highlighted how MeMed BV not only outperforms existing practices but also contributes to reducing healthcare costs.

news.headline: Kidney Stone Retrieval Devices Market to exceed $4.8B by 2034, with a CAGR of 5.7%. Major players include Boston Scientific, Cook Medical, and Olympus. Advancements in minimally invasive procedures and technology are driving growth.
news.summary: The global Kidney Stone Retrieval Devices industry was valued at US$ 2.6 billion in 2023 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of 5.7% from 2024 to 2034, reaching around US$ 4.8 billion by 2034. Key factors driving this growth include the increasing prevalence of kidney stone diseases, advancements in minimally invasive procedures, and rising healthcare expenditure globally. Major players in the market include Boston Scientific Corporation, Cook Medical Inc., Olympus Corporation, and Stryker Corporation. These companies are focusing on research and development to introduce innovative products, mergers, acquisitions, and strategic collaborations to strengthen their market presence. Advancements in the industry include innovations in lithotripsy technology, integration of AI, and increased adoption of minimally invasive procedures.

news.headline: Mortgage rates drop to 6.76%, lowest in over two months, positive sign for home buyers.
news.summary: Freddie Mac, based in McLean, Virginia, shared data on Feb. 27, 2025, revealing that the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage (FRM) had an average rate of 6.76%. Chief Economist Sam Khater noted that this rate was the lowest in over two months. The decrease in rates, coupled with a slight increase in housing inventory, is seen as positive news for potential homebuyers. This update was part of Freddie Mac's Primary Mortgage Market Survey® (PMMS®). More details are accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Pending home sales hit record low, jobless claims soar, and GDP revised up minimally; inflation signs apparent with 2.4-2.7% increase.
news.summary: Wall Street is processing a variety of economic reports on Thursday, including updates on pending home sales, jobless claims, and fourth quarter GDP figures. The U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis released the second estimate for fourth quarter 2024 real GDP, showing a minor upward revision of less than 0.1 percentage point. This adjustment was primarily attributed to higher-than-anticipated government spending and exports. The data also revealed indications of mounting inflation, with price indexes closely monitored by the Federal Reserve being adjusted upwards. The personal consumption expenditures price index for the quarter demonstrated a 2.4% increase, revised up by 0.1 percentage point, and a 2.7% gain, revised up by 0.2 percentage points when excluding food and energy. This information is crucial for investors in dissecting market trends and making informed decisions.

news.headline: Fed's Hammack says 2% inflation is not in sight, interest rates to stay unchanged, job market strong, inflation progress uneven.
news.summary: The Federal Reserve, led by Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack, is expected to keep interest rates constant in the near future. This decision comes as policymakers analyze inflation patterns and the status of the labor market. Hammack stressed the importance of patience during a recent announcement, noting that despite some advancements, inflation has yet to consistently reach the 2% goal. Following recent policy talks, the Federal Reserve indicated that interest rates will probably stay the same for an extended timeframe. While the job market shows strength, inflation has not uniformly decreased.

news.headline: Markets fluctuate amid tariff concerns, stagnant economic growth. S&P 500 and Nasdaq dip as Dow Jones stays up. Rising jobless claims and low pending home sales hint at economic caution. GDP shows signs of inflation with core PCE increases. Analyst warns of potential trouble ahead in US economy.
news.summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq reversed earlier gains to fall into negative territory, while the Dow Jones remained positive in midday trading. NVIDIA Corp. (NASDAQ: NVDA) led the tech sector down after reporting its smallest revenue beat in years. Jobless claims surged and pending home sales hit a record low in January. The second estimate of Q4 2024 real GDP showed signs of increasing inflation, with the PCE price index revised up by 0.1 percentage points and core PCE revised up by 0.2 percentage points. Economist Mohamed A. El-Erian highlighted the GDP revisions, rising jobless claims, and declining pending home sales as potential warning signs. Tesla faces tough competition from Chinese rivals, according to an analyst.

news.headline: Bitcoin's $1.5 trillion market cap overshadowed its DeFi utility. Solv Protocol aims to bridge Bitcoin to DeFi with $160M trading volume, highlighting industry shift towards Bitcoin-driven DeFi applications. Hindered by 4-year delay in Ethereum-based DeFi maturation, Bitcoin's potential unlocked by recent regulatory thaw..ToDecimal291
news.summary: Bitcoin, with a market capitalization of $1.5 trillion, surpasses its competitors but lags in decentralized finance (DeFi) utility due to slow infrastructure growth and regulatory challenges, as per Ryan Chow of Solv Protocol. Solv, facilitating Bitcoin to DeFi with a $160 million trading volume, highlights Ethereum-based DeFi maturing in four years and recent regulatory changes as key factors in unlocking Bitcoin’s potential. The industry is seen transitioning towards a Bitcoin-driven transformation fueled by lending and staking applications. Interviewed at Eth Denver by Benzinga, Chow attributes Bitcoin's sluggish DeFi progress to foundational obstacles, projecting potential unlocking not until 2024, requiring time for DeFi development.

news.headline: Hasbro's Transformers action figure wins 2025 TOTY Award for Action Figure of the Year in New York City gala.
news.summary: Hasbro, Inc., a prominent games, IP, and toy company based in Pawtucket, Rhode Island, revealed that their Transformers One Power Flip Optimus Prime (Orion Pax) action figure secured the prestigious Action Figure of the Year award at the TOTY Awards gala in New York City on February 28. This action figure, which seamlessly transforms between four modes, is inspired by the beloved character from Paramount Animation and Hasbro’s Transformers One movie. The win was acknowledged as a significant achievement by the company.

news.headline: Danny Ryan, an Ethereum developer, appointed co-founder of Etherealize. Also chosen informally as the head of the Ethereum Foundation.
news.summary: Recently, the Ethereum developer has been informally selected as the leader of the Ethereum Foundation in a vote that is not official or binding.

news.headline: Toy trends for 2025 revealed at Toy Fair® in New York, include "Newstalgia Trip", "Collectible Cravings", and "It's My Vibe".
news.summary: In New York on March 1, 2025, The Toy Association™ revealed the anticipated trends for 2025 at the 119th Toy Fair® in the Javits Center. These trends, including "Newstalgia Trip," "Collectible Cravings," and "It's My Vibe," are expected to shape the toy and play industry moving forward. This announcement comes as part of the event that showcases the latest innovations and products in the toy sector. Attendees can expect to see an array of new and exciting offerings aligned with these emerging themes.

news.headline: Crypto trader lost $15.68 million trading MELANIA Memecoin; another suffered a $3.73 million loss this week. Whales sold off their holdings.
news.summary: An individual crypto trader recently experienced a significant loss of $15.68 million while trading the official memecoin associated with First Lady Melania Trump. The trader had purchased around 14 million MELANIA tokens last month for an estimated $30 million, only to sell all holdings this week resulting in the substantial loss. In a separate incident, another trader faced a loss of $3.73 million in their MELANIA trade. This data was revealed by the blockchain tracking company Lookonchain. Notably, whales also sold off their MELANIA holdings at a loss recently. More details on this occurrence are accessible on various news platforms.

news.headline: In 2025, ICOminer's app offers Bitcoin cloud mining with investments starting at $200, earning up to $2970 daily.
news.summary: London UK, March 01, 2025 - Cloud mining has emerged as the preferred method for earning Bitcoin among individuals and investors, eliminating the need for costly hardware or specialized knowledge. In 2025, one of the leading platforms is ICOminer, known for its lucrative investment schemes, secure transactions, and user-friendly mobile application. With ICOminer, starting Bitcoin mining requires just $200, potentially leading to daily earnings of up to $2970. Offering a range of investment plans suitable for novices and experienced investors, ICOminer promises daily rebates and ensures principal refunds upon contract conclusion. New users can also receive a bonus of $50. The investment plans are categorized based on investment amount, contract duration, daily earnings, and total returns. For instance, the Newbie plan involves a $200 investment for 1 day, yielding $6.00 daily and $206.00 in total returns. The Ethereum Classic plan, requiring a $600 investment over 3 days, generates $10.20 daily and $630.60 in total returns. Similarly, the Litecoin plan involves a $1,200 investment for 5 days, providing $22.80 daily and $1,314.00 in total returns. Lastly, the Bitcoin plan necessitates an $8,800 investment over 10 days, resulting in a daily return of $220.00.

news.headline: Trump's tariffs lead to $1 trillion crypto market crash; Bitcoin drops to below $80,000, down 28% from $109,241.
news.summary: The cryptocurrency market cap has decreased by $1 trillion since December 2024 due to President Donald Trump's proposed tariffs. This prompted a significant sell-off with Bitcoin dropping below $80,000, a 28% decrease from its peak of $109,241 in January. Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency, also saw a decline, hitting its lowest value since January 2024. President Trump, previously known as the "crypto president," revealed intentions to enforce 25% tariffs on Mexico and Canada starting Tuesday, causing market turmoil.

news.headline: Katz Issues Statement on Jarmana Druze
Researchers develop water-harvesting tech from kitchen scraps & seashells, producing 4 gallons daily w/ low energy.
news.summary: Scientists at a university have successfully transformed common waste into sorbents that can generate approximately 4 gallons of water each day, requiring very little energy.

news.headline: Israel may protect Syrian town Jarmana from government forces: Katz
Jasper Therapeutics presents briquilimab data at AAAAI Annual Meeting, including Phase 1b/2a BEACON study results, with 4 preclinical studies, held in San Diego from Feb 28 - March 3, 2025.
news.summary: On March 01, 2025, Jasper Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: JSPR) shared updated clinical data at the AAAAI 2025 Annual Meeting in San Diego, CA. The company is focusing on developing briquilimab, an antibody therapy targeting c-Kit for mast cell driven diseases like chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU), chronic inducible urticaria (CIndU), and asthma. The data presented included findings from the Phase 1b/2a BEACON study and four preclinical studies on briquilimab. The event, held from February 28 to March 3, 2025, showcased the progress of Jasper in this area.

news.headline: Milei's 'Libragate': LIBRA post raised prices, then plummeted, causing investor losses. Legal trouble for Milei.
news.summary: The prices of LIBRA cryptocurrency experienced a significant surge following Milei's social media post, only to sharply decline afterwards. This sudden turn of events has resulted in investors grappling with substantial financial losses, while Milei finds himself entangled in a web of legal challenges and political repercussions.

news.headline: Andrew Cuomo: running for mayor of New York City
Crypto analyst Michaël van de Poppe predicts a 195% Bitcoin rally, anticipating it could reach up to $250,000 in the current cycle. Bull market for Bitcoin still strong.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency analyst Michaël van de Poppe remains optimistic about the bull market for Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC). Despite a recent correction bringing the cryptocurrency to the $80,000 range, van de Poppe predicts a significant rally ahead. He anticipates that Bitcoin could potentially reach a high of $250,000 in this cycle, representing a 195% increase from its current value. Van de Poppe expressed his belief that the cycle has just begun and that Bitcoin has not yet reached a state of euphoric hype, suggesting that the cryptocurrency could surpass $200,000 to $250,000 in the near future.

news.headline: Andrew Cuomo joins race for New York City Mayor, NYT
Telegram fined A$1 million for delayed response to safety regulations, Apple praised by Trump for $500 billion investment.
news.summary: President Donald Trump recently praised Apple Inc. for its massive $500 billion investment in the U.S. tech industry. On a different note in the technology sector, Australia's online safety regulator ordered messaging platform Telegram to pay a fine of approximately 1 million Australian dollars ($640,000) for delays in responding to an inquiry regarding the prevention of child abuse and extremist content. In another development, AMC Entertainment Holdings, Inc. CEO Adam Aron mentioned that major streamers like Apple and Amazon.com are reconsidering their strategies for the big screen. These stories highlight significant financial investments and regulatory actions in the technology and entertainment sectors.

news.headline: AgriBank reported strong financial results for Q4 2024 and full year, with $927.5 million net income, 99.4% acceptable loans, and $164.7 billion total loans. CEO Jeffrey Swanhorst emphasized the financial strength, stakeholder satisfaction, and commitment to supporting rural communities and agriculture.
news.summary: AgriBank, based in St. Paul, had a strong financial performance in 2024. Net income was $927.5 million, with a return on assets ratio of 51 basis points. The credit quality remained high, with 99.4% of loans classified as acceptable. Liquidity was strong at 152 days, exceeding regulatory requirements. Net interest income was $1.1 billion, an increase of 7.8% from the prior year. Total loans reached $164.7 billion, up 10.7% from 2023. AgriBank's CEO attributed success to the Funding Bank Model and Farm Credit Association-owners. The U.S. Department of Agriculture forecasted a decline in net farm income for 2024.

news.headline: NATO's Rutte advises Zelenskiy to mend ties with Trump amid Ukraine conflict.
news.summary: A recent report indicates a surge in global COVID-19 cases, with more than 5 million confirmed infections and over 200,000 deaths worldwide. The United States has been the hardest hit, with over 1.3 million cases and 70,000 deaths. Brazil follows with more than 200,000 cases and 15,000 deaths. The pandemic continues to impact countries across the globe, with concerns growing about the long-term effects on public health and the economy. Efforts to flatten the curve and develop a vaccine are ongoing, but the situation remains fluid.

news.headline: Coinbase triumphs with public keys, Marathon faces criticism as Bitcoin faces volatility. Analysts not impressed.
news.summary: Analysts closely monitoring Marathon expected positive feedback for the company, given its performance. However, the response has not been as favorable as anticipated.
